intro.title: Because it is there
intro.body: The 1924 British Everest expedition was the third attempt on the mountain. George Mallory and Andrew Irvine left Camp VI for the summit on the morning of 8 June and were last seen 'going strong for the top' by Noel Odell at around 12:50 pm. Mallory's body was found in 1999; whether they reached the top is unknown.

stops.name: Darjeeling
stops.note: Expedition assembles February-March 1924. Tibet permits, Sherpa hiring, Howard-Bury and Norton in command.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Hill-station town with toy-train rails, snow Kangchenjunga in the distance, expedition kit in canvas piles on the verandah.

stops.name: Phari Dzong
stops.note: Tibetan plateau staging post, end of March 1924. The expedition crosses into Tibet via the Jelep La.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Brown-mud fort on a high windswept plain, yaks loaded with kit, expedition members in tweeds.

stops.name: Rongbuk Monastery
stops.note: Base of the approach. Lama Dzatrul receives the expedition; Mallory describes the monastery in letters home.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Whitewashed monastery on a stony plain with Everest rising behind, monks blowing long horns.

stops.name: Camp V (North Col)
stops.note: Snow-camp on the col between Everest and Changtse, 7,000 m. The launch point for the summit push, 6 June 1924.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Two small canvas tents in a snow saddle between two black peaks, climbers in tweed and silk in front.

stops.name: Camp VI / North-east Ridge
stops.note: 8 June 1924. Mallory and Irvine leave for the summit at 8 am with oxygen sets. Odell sees them at 12:50 pm 'going strong for the top'. They never return.
stops.imagePrompt: Sepia plate. Two figures roped together on a high snow ridge, oxygen apparatus on their backs, summit pyramid above, swirling clouds.
